body:
This PR:

 - adds some unit tests and some comments on some thread-block configurations that could be improved.
 - Slightly refactors the `masked_partition` so that we can more easily unit-test it
 - Renames `fd_stencil_partition` to `fd_shmem_stencil_partition`

It's unfortunate that https://github.com/maleadt/StaticCartesian.jl doesn't work. If it did, we could basically eliminate all of these functions and just use `linear_partition` + `CartesianIndices` with static ranges, and we would get fairly good performance for all of our kernels.

Instead, we occasionally get 2x slower with `linear_partition`, and need to carefully tune each of our `partition` methods.

Once we merge this, I can fix some of the configurations that result in the expensive surface kernels.

Also, it looks like we could even improve performance for the masked partition. So, that could give us a significant boost.
title: Add performance tuning tests

body:
`Remapping.Remapper` performs spectral interpolation. When interpolating, every point in a given element is used to calculate the target value. As a result, the result is incorrect if one or more of the nodes in an element are masked.

This commit prevents users from accidentally obtaining subtly incorrect results by calling the Remapper on a space that is not supported.

Note that if none of the nodes in an element are masked, the interpolation routine would be fine. A more refined implementation of this check could take this into consideration, but this is deferred to when such need arises.
title: Prevent mask from being incorrectly used when Remapping on a non-supported space
